$AAPL is making Messages more context-aware with Apple Intelligence.

The app can now suggest actions like adding a photo, note or reminder based on the conversation you are having.

$AAPL announced new Apple Foundation Models with $GOOGL built to run smaller AI models both on-device and in the cloud.

The upgrade is aimed at improving image understanding, generation, dictation, speech, personal search and web-connected Apple Intelligence.

Cathie Wood said $SPCX has “10-year lead” in reusable rockets while competitors like Blue Origin are still struggling to relaunch successfully.

She also said Starlink’s profitability could be “astonishing” as SpaceX scales satellite internet business.

$NVDA CEO Jensen Huang said “buy $QCOM stock” while discussing edge AI and the need for AI chips inside smartphones.

Qualcomm is positioned to benefit as more AI inference moves to devices.

Billionaire investor Ron Baron says he placed a $1B order for $SPCX IPO shares and has no plans to sell in his lifetime.

He believes SpaceX can grow to ~$20T over the next 10-15 years with Starlink alone potentially doing $1T in annual revenue.

$NVDA is reportedly testing $INTC 18A process and EMIB packaging for its next-gen Feynman processor.

The main GPU compute die would still stay with $TSM but Intel could win share in advanced packaging and I/O where capacity is becoming a real bottleneck

$GOOGL reportedly ordered $INTC to package more than 3M TPUs.

Google is expected to produce 6M+ TPUs using Intel’s advanced packaging technology across 2027 and 2028.
The Semiconductor Index $SOXX is opening up over 5% after posting its worst trading day in six years last Friday.

$GOOGL and $NVDA are reportedly considering $INTC as a backup manufacturer for advanced AI chips as $TSM capacity remains tight.

Major AI chip designers are exploring Intel foundry as demand overwhelms leading-edge supply

$AMZN announced a multibillion-dollar deal with $GLW to supply optical fiber, cable and connectivity solutions for its U.S. data center buildout.

The deal expands Amazon’s domestic fiber supply chain as AI data center demand keeps scaling

$NVDA CEO Jensen Huang says partnerships with SK Hynix & SK Telecom could bring hundreds of billions of dollars of business to South Korea.

He also said SK Hynix doubling wafer capacity by 2030 still “is not enough” which tells you how extreme AI memory demand has become.

Bernstein estimates a $NVDA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack will cost ~$9.1M with HBM4 pricing rising to ~$53/GB by 2027 as volumes ramp.

That would put memory/storage near ~$3.2M per rack and imply a major HBM revenue step-up for $MU, Samsung and SK Hynix.

Since the War began on Feb 27th, the S&P 500 (black) is up 7.34%. But the S&P 500 without AI stocks (blue) is effectively unchanged. So, the entire S&P 500 Index rally since February has been driven by AI stocks.

But look at what happened on Friday, June 5th!

The S&P 500 was down 2.6%, its biggest daily loss since last October.

The S&P 500 without AI was up 0.02% (call it unchanged). 

So, the entire sell-off on Friday was AI stocks. The "normal world" did nothing on Friday.

A thought

In the 1950s to the 1970s, information channels were so scarce that even the most studious investor, reading the same New York Times as everyone else on the morning commute, inevitably absorbed the same narrative, producing classic groupthink. 

Today, I fear we might be recreating that exact dynamic at digital speed: millions of users generate daily AI briefings with near-identical prompts fed into overlapping models, receiving essentially the same market summaries, signals, and conclusions.  

The result? A new era of synchronized thinking, just like 50–70 years ago, when alpha generation was far higher precisely because consensus created exploitable edges. 

Independent thinkers who step outside the AI echo chamber will soon regain that same advantage. 

Active stock-picking is poised for a comeback.
----
The era of Google searches, circa 2005 to 2024, was always ad hoc, so they never produced groupthink on the level we saw in the 1950s, and may see again.

The global memory market is now projected to reach as much as $889.3 billion in 2026, per TrendForce, up 296% YoY and a substantial 61.2% increase from its prior forecast for $551.6 billion.

$MU $AMD $NVDA $AVGO

Most indices paused into resistance#1

📺 HOW TO TRADE THE BOUNCE AFTER A BIG SELLOFF

After a sharp selloff, the biggest mistake traders can make is assuming they know what comes next.

While Friday's decline created fear, it also created opportunity—but only for those willing to stay patient and focus on key levels. 

The focus now should be on identifying where a relief rally could run into resistance and where sellers may reappear.

*

For $SPY, the 21-day near $743 is a critical level to watch. For $QQQ, that level sits near $716. 

A bounce into those areas would be a logical retracement after the selloff, but they could also become zones where profit-taking and renewed selling pressure emerge.

*

I am cautious against automatically shorting the first bounce, as markets often recover further than expected and can squeeze traders positioned too aggressively. 

At the same time, I am against chasing strength, especially after a sharp decline like on Friday.

For traders who held too much exposure during the pullback, a bounce may offer an opportunity to reduce risk, clean up positions, and improve portfolio construction. 

For those coming in flat, patience may be the best strategy until the market reveals its next direction.

*

I want to remind you that pullbacks are a normal part of investing. 

Historically, annual market declines are common even during strong long-term bull markets. Consistently adding capital during periods of weakness can improve long-term returns and lower average cost over time.

*

For now, I have reduced equity exposure, maintained some hedges, and am approaching the market tactically. 

My goal is not to force trades but to let opportunities develop while monitoring which stocks and sectors show relative strength during the correction.

So, don't chase, don't panic, don't assume Friday was the final low. 

Watch the bounce, respect resistance, manage risk, and stay patiently aggressive until higher-probability setups emerge.

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

Goldman no longer expects the Fed to cut rates this year after stronger-than-expected labor data.

The bank pushed its final two expected cuts to June and December 2027, from December 2026 and March 2027.

Goldman still views hikes as unlikely, but raised the probability of modest hikes to 20% from 10%.
